Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

CTN 4, 199 [SB Gilgameš 01][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
Column i
(start of column missing)
o i 1'o 1'

[... ḫi]-⸢iṭ⸣-[ma ...]

Survey [...] and [...]!

o i 2'2'

[x x] ⸢SIG₄⸣-šu la ⸢a⸣-[gur-rat]

[...] its brickwork is not kiln-fired brick,

o i 3'3'

[...] ⸢id⸣-du-u₂ ⸢7⸣ [...]

[...] the 7 [...] did [...] lay [...]!

o i 4'4'

[... giš]⸢KIRI₆⸣-MEŠ šar₂ es-su-⸢u₂⸣ pi-⸢tir⸣ [...]

[...] is date-grove, a šāru-measure is clay-pit, a pitru-measure is [...]:

o i 5'5'

[x x] u₃ pi-ti-ir ⸢UNUG⸣ki ⸢tam⸣-[ši-ḫu]

[...] and a pitru-measure is Uruk, the measurement.

o i 6'6'

[x x] gištup-šen-na ša₂ ⸢giš⸣[EREN]

[...] the tablet-box of cedar,

o i 7'7'

[pu?-uṭ?]-⸢ṭer?⸣ ḫar-gal-li-šu ša₂ ⸢ZABAR⸣

release its clasps of bronze!

o i 8'8'

[pi?-te?]-⸢ma?⸣ KA₂ ša₂ ni-ṣir-ti-[šu₂]

Open the lid of its secret,

o i 9'9'

[i?]-⸢ši?⸣-ma ṭup-pi ⸢na₄⸣ZA.GIN₃ ši-tas-si

lift up the tablet of lapis lazuli and read aloud

o i 10'10'

[mim-mu]-u₂ dGIŠ-gim₂-maš DU.DUku ka-lu mar-ṣa-a-ti

all the hardships, all that Gilgameš kept going through!

o i 11'11'

[šu]-⸢tu⸣-ur UGU LUGAL-MEŠ ša₂-nu-ʾu-u₂-du EN gat-ti

He surpasses (other) kings, brave, powerfully built,

o i 12'12'

⸢qar⸣-du lil-lid UNUGki ri-i-mu mut-tak-pu

hero, native of Uruk, butting wild bull!

o i 13'13'

⸢il⸣-lak ina pa-ni a-ša₂-red

He goes at the fore as the leader,

o i 14'14'

⸢ar⸣-ka ⸢il⸣-lak-ma tukul-ti ŠEŠ-MEŠ-šu₂

he also goes at the rear as the trust of his brothers!

o i 15'15'

⸢kib⸣-ru dan-nu ṣu-lul um-ma-ni-šu₂

A strong bank, the protection of his army,

o i 16'16'

⸢a-gu⸣-u₂ ez-zu mu-ab-bit BAD₃ NA₄

a violent wave, the one who destroys a wall of stone!

o i 17'17'

[ri]-⸢mu⸣ ša₂ dLUGAL.BAN₃.DA dGIŠ-gim₂-maš git-⸢ma⸣-lu e-mu-qi₂

Wild bull of Lugalbanda, Gilgameš, perfect in strength,

o i 18'18'

[e]-⸢niq⸣ ar-ḫi ṣir-ti fri-mat—dNIN.SUN₂

suckling of the exalted cow, Rimat-Ninsun!

o i 19'19'

[ši]-⸢ḫu⸣ dGIŠ-gim₂-maš git₂-ma-lu ra-šub-bu

Gilgameš so tall, perfect, terrifying,

o i 20'20'

[pe]-⸢tu⸣-u₂ ne₂-re-be₂-e-ti ša₂ ḫur-sa-a-ni

the one who opens mountain passes;

o i 21'21'

[ḫe]-⸢ru⸣-u₂ bu-⸢u₂⸣-ri ša₂ ⸢GU₂⸣ KURi

the one who digs mountainside wells,

o i 22'22'

[e]-⸢bir⸣ a-ab-⸢ba ta⸣-ma-ti DAGALti EN dUTU.E₃

the one who crosses the ocean, the wide sea, as far as the sunrise;

o i 23'23'

[ḫa-a]-a-iṭ kib-ra-a-ti muš-⸢te-ʾu⸣-u₂ ba-la₂-ṭi

the one who surveys the world-regions, the one who keeps searching for life,

o i 24'24'

[ka]-⸢ši⸣-id ⸢dan⸣-nu-⸢us⸣-su a-na mUD—ZI ⸢ru⸣-u₂-⸢qi₂⸣

the one who reaches through his strength Uta-napišti, the distant;

o i 25'25'

[mu]-⸢ter ma-ḫa-zi⸣ ana aš-ri-šu₂-nu ša₂ u₂-ḫal-li-qu a-bu-bu

the one who restores cult-centres that the flood destroyed,

o i 26'26'

[mu]-⸢kin par-ṣi⸣ ana UN-MEŠ a-pa-a-ti

the one who establishes cultic regulations for the numerous people!

o i 27'27'

[x x] ⸢it-ti⸣-šu iš-ša₂-an-na-nu a-na LUGALti

[...] can be compared with him for kingship?

o i 28'28'

[...] ⸢d⸣GIŠ-gim₂-maš i-qab-bu-u₂ a-na-ku-ma LUGAL

[...] can say [...] Gilgameš: 'I am the king'?

o i 29'29'

[dGIŠ-gim₂]-⸢maš ul⸣-tu u₄-um ⸢iʾ⸣-al-du na-bu šum-šu₂

Gilgameš was his name from the day he was born,

o i 30'30'

[šit-tin]-⸢šu₂⸣ DINGIR-ma šul-lul-ta-šu₂ a-me-lu-tu

two-thirds of him god but one-third of him human.

o i 31'31'

[x x pag]-⸢ri⸣-šu DINGIR.MAḪ ⸢uṣ-ṣir⸣

Belet-ili drew [...] of his body,

o i 32'32'

[...] gat-⸢ta⸣-šu₂ ⸢dNU⸣.DIM₂.[MUD]

Nudimmud [...] his build.

o i 33'33'

[...] x NA ša₂-ru-uḫ [...]

[...] ... was splendid [...],

o i 34'34'

[...] ⸢la-na⸣ x x U₂ [...]

[...] stature ... [...].

o i 35'35'

[...] bi-rit [...]

[...] distance between [...]

o i 36'36'

[...] x x [...]

[...] ... [...]

(rest of column missing)
Column ii
(start of column missing)
o ii 1'o 1'

⸢it⸣-[ti ...]

With [...],

o ii 2'2'

⸢it-ti⸣ [...]

with [...].

o ii 3'3'

ṣa-⸢a⸣-[a-du ...]

A hunter [...],

o ii 4'4'

ina ⸢pu⸣-[ut ...]

beside [...].

o ii 5'5'

1[en ...]

One [...].

(4 lines missing)
o ii 10'10'

⸢i⸣-[ba-aš₂-ši ...]

There was [...],

o ii 11'11'

a-na [...]

to [...].

o ii 12'12'

ṣa-⸢a⸣-a-[du ...]

The hunter [...]:

o ii 13'13'

a-bi 1[en ...]

'My father, one [...].

o ii 14'14'

ina ⸢ma⸣-[ti ...]

'In the land [...],
